info.name: Results Service API
info.description: Complete API collection for the Results Service - a comprehensive test execution management system. This service manages test runs, individual tests, and their results within QA projects. All endpoints require authentication via Bearer token.

request.description: Health check endpoint to verify service availability. Returns a status object indicating the service is operational. This endpoint does not require authentication.

item.name: Runs

body.raw:
{
  "projectId": "507f1f77bcf86cd799439011",
  "name": "Regression Test Run Q1 2026",
  "description": "Full regression suite for release 2.5.0",
  "type": "Automated",
  "status": "Pending",
  "environment": "staging",
  "executedBy": "507f1f77bcf86cd799439012",
  "startTime": "2026-01-20T10:00:00.000Z"
}

request.description:
Creates a new test run within a project. A run represents a collection of tests executed together as part of a test cycle.

**Required Fields:**
- `projectId` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the project
- `name` (string, min 2 chars): Name of the test run

**Optional Fields:**
- `description` (string): Detailed description of the run purpose
- `type` (enum): Run type - "Manual", "Automated", or "Scheduled" (default: "Automated")
- `status` (enum): Current status - "Pending", "Running", "Completed", or "Aborted" (default: "Pending")
- `environment` (string): Target environment (e.g., dev, staging, production)
- `executedBy` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the user executing the run
- `startTime` (date): When the run started
- `endTime` (date): When the run completed

**Auto-calculated:**
- `duration` (number): Automatically calculated in seconds from startTime and endTime
- `isActive` (boolean): Default true
- `createdAt`, `updatedAt`: Timestamps

Retrieves all test runs for a specific project. Returns an array of run objects with complete details including execution metadata, status, and timing information.

**Path Parameters:**
- `projectId` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the project

**Response includes:**
- Run name and description
- Type and status
- Environment configuration
- Executor information
- Timing and duration details
- Active status and timestamps

Alternative endpoint to retrieve all runs for a specific project. Functionally identical to GET /runs/:projectId but uses a more explicit path structure.

**Path Parameters:**
- `projectId` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the project

**Use Case:** Use this endpoint when you prefer explicit resource naming or when integrating with systems that expect /project/ in the path.

Retrieves a single test run by its unique identifier. Returns complete run details including all metadata, timing information, and execution status.

**Path Parameters:**
- `id` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the run

**Returns:**
- Complete run object with all fields
- Populated references (if configured)
- Creation and modification timestamps

**Error Responses:**
- 404: Run not found
- 401: Unauthorized

Updates an existing test run. All fields are optional - only include fields you want to modify.

**Path Parameters:**
- `id` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the run to update

**Updateable Fields:**
- `name` (string, min 2 chars): Run name
- `description` (string): Run description
- `type` (enum): "Manual", "Automated", or "Scheduled"
- `status` (enum): "Pending", "Running", "Completed", or "Aborted"
- `environment` (string): Target environment
- `executedBy` (string): User ObjectId
- `startTime` (date): Start timestamp
- `endTime` (date): End timestamp
- `isActive` (boolean): Active status

**Common Use Cases:**
- Update status as run progresses
- Set end time and mark as completed
- Mark run as aborted
- Update environment or executor information

Deletes a test run from the system. This operation may be a soft delete (setting isActive to false) or hard delete depending on service configuration.

**Path Parameters:**
- `id` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the run to delete

**Important Notes:**
- Deleting a run may affect associated tests and results
- Consider the impact on historical reporting before deleting
- Some implementations may prevent deletion of runs with associated data

**Error Responses:**
- 404: Run not found
- 401: Unauthorized
- 403: Forbidden (insufficient permissions)

Generic search endpoint for runs using the prime-qa-commons search functionality. Allows flexible querying across run fields.

**Query Parameters:**
- `query` (string): Search term to match
- `fields` (string): Comma-separated list of fields to search in
- Additional filters supported by the generic search implementation

**Searchable Fields:**
- name
- description
- type
- status
- environment

**Use Cases:**
- Find runs by name pattern
- Search runs by environment
- Filter runs by type or status

item.description: Test Run management endpoints. Runs represent test execution cycles within a project, containing metadata about when tests were executed, by whom, and in what environment.

item.name: Tests

request.description:
Creates a new test instance within a test run. A test represents an individual test case that will be executed as part of a run.

**Required Fields:**
- `runId` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the parent run
- `testCaseId` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the test case definition
- `title` (string, min 2 chars): Descriptive title of the test

**Optional Fields:**
- `projectId` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the project (auto-populated from run if not provided)
- `suiteId` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the test suite
- `sectionId` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the test section
- `status` (enum): "Not Started", "In Progress", or "Completed" (default: "Not Started")

**Auto-generated:**
- `isActive` (boolean): Default true
- `createdAt`, `updatedAt`: Timestamps

**Relationships:**
- Links to Run (execution context)
- Links to TestCase (test definition)
- Optionally links to Suite and Section (organization)

Retrieves all tests across all runs. Returns a comprehensive list of test instances with their associated run, project, and test case information.

**Response includes:**
- Test title and identifiers
- Run and project associations
- Test case, suite, and section references
- Status and timestamps
- Active status

**Use Cases:**
- Overview of all test instances
- Bulk data export
- Cross-run analysis

**Note:** For large datasets, consider using filtered endpoints like /tests/run/:runId or /tests/project/:projectId instead.

Retrieves all tests associated with a specific run. This is the primary way to get the list of tests included in a test execution cycle.

**Path Parameters:**
- `runId` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the run

**Response includes:**
- All tests belonging to the specified run
- Test metadata and associations
- Current status of each test

**Use Cases:**
- Display tests in a run
- Monitor run progress
- Execute tests from a run
- Generate run reports

Retrieves all test instances for a specific project across all runs. Useful for project-level reporting and analysis.

**Path Parameters:**
- `projectId` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the project

**Response includes:**
- All tests from all runs in the project
- Complete test metadata
- Run associations

**Use Cases:**
- Project-wide test execution history
- Cross-run test analysis
- Test coverage reporting
- Historical execution data

Retrieves a single test instance by its unique identifier. Returns complete test details including all associations and metadata.

**Path Parameters:**
- `id` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the test

**Returns:**
- Complete test object with all fields
- Run, project, and test case associations
- Suite and section references
- Timestamps and status

**Error Responses:**
- 404: Test not found
- 401: Unauthorized

Updates an existing test instance. Primarily used to update test status as it progresses through execution.

**Path Parameters:**
- `id` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the test to update

**Updateable Fields:**
- `title` (string, min 2 chars): Test title
- `status` (enum): "Not Started", "In Progress", or "Completed"
- `isActive` (boolean): Active status

**Common Use Cases:**
- Mark test as in progress when execution starts
- Mark test as completed when execution finishes
- Update test title if needed
- Deactivate tests

**Note:** Only includes fields that are expected to change. Run, project, and test case associations are typically not modified after creation.

Deletes a test instance from a run. May be a soft delete (isActive = false) or hard delete based on configuration.

**Path Parameters:**
- `id` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the test to delete

**Important Considerations:**
- May affect associated results
- Consider impact on run completeness
- May affect reporting and metrics

**Use Cases:**
- Remove tests added to run by mistake
- Clean up duplicate test instances
- Remove obsolete tests

**Error Responses:**
- 404: Test not found
- 401: Unauthorized
- 403: Forbidden

Generic search endpoint for tests using prime-qa-commons search functionality. Enables flexible querying across test fields.

**Query Parameters:**
- `query` (string): Search term to match
- `fields` (string): Comma-separated list of fields to search in
- Additional filters supported by generic search

**Searchable Fields:**
- title
- status
- And other indexed fields

**Use Cases:**
- Find tests by title pattern
- Filter tests by status
- Search across multiple fields

item.description: Test management endpoints. Tests represent individual test case instances within a run, linking test definitions to execution cycles.

item.name: Results

body.raw:
{
  "testId": "507f1f77bcf86cd799439017",
  "projectId": "507f1f77bcf86cd799439011",
  "status": "Passed",
  "executedBy": "507f1f77bcf86cd799439012",
  "startTime": "2026-01-20T10:35:00.000Z",
  "endTime": "2026-01-20T10:36:30.000Z",
  "logs": "Test executed successfully. All assertions passed. Response time: 245ms"
}

request.description:
Creates a new test execution result. Results capture the outcome of executing a test, including status, timing, and execution details.

**Required Fields:**
- `testId` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the test that was executed

**Optional Fields:**
- `projectId` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the project (auto-populated from test if not provided)
- `status` (enum): "Passed", "Failed", "Blocked", "Skipped", or "Retest" (default: "Skipped")
- `executedBy` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the user who executed the test
- `startTime` (date): When test execution started
- `endTime` (date): When test execution completed
- `logs` (string): Detailed execution logs, error messages, or notes

**Auto-calculated Fields:**
- `duration` (number): Automatically calculated in seconds from startTime and endTime (via pre-save hook)
- `isActive` (boolean): Default true
- `createdAt`, `updatedAt`: Timestamps

**Status Values:**
- **Passed**: Test executed successfully with all assertions passing
- **Failed**: Test executed but one or more assertions failed
- **Blocked**: Test could not execute due to blocker (environment issue, dependency failure)
- **Skipped**: Test was intentionally skipped
- **Retest**: Test needs to be re-executed

Retrieves all test results across all tests and runs. Returns comprehensive result data including execution outcomes, timing, and logs.

**Response includes:**
- Test and project associations
- Execution status
- Timing information (start, end, duration)
- Executor details
- Execution logs
- Timestamps

**Use Cases:**
- System-wide result analysis
- Bulk data export
- Cross-project reporting
- Audit trail

**Note:** For production systems with large datasets, prefer filtered endpoints like /results/test/:testId or /results/project/:projectId

Retrieves all execution results for a specific test. Shows the complete execution history including multiple attempts, retests, and historical data.

**Path Parameters:**
- `testId` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the test

**Response includes:**
- All result records for the test
- Chronological execution history
- Status progression
- Timing and duration data

**Use Cases:**
- View test execution history
- Analyze test stability/flakiness
- Review previous execution attempts
- Debug test failures with logs
- Track retest attempts

Retrieves all test execution results for a specific project. Provides project-level execution metrics and history.

**Path Parameters:**
- `projectId` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the project

**Response includes:**
- All results from all tests in the project
- Complete execution history
- Cross-run result data

**Use Cases:**
- Project-level reporting
- Quality metrics calculation (pass rate, execution time)
- Trend analysis over time
- Test execution coverage
- Performance monitoring

Retrieves a single test result by its unique identifier. Returns complete execution details for a specific result instance.

**Path Parameters:**
- `id` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the result

**Returns:**
- Complete result object with all fields
- Test and project associations
- Full execution details
- Timing information
- Execution logs
- Timestamps

**Use Cases:**
- View detailed execution information
- Retrieve specific result for reporting
- Debug specific test execution
- Link to result from external systems

**Error Responses:**
- 404: Result not found
- 401: Unauthorized

Updates an existing test result. Typically used to modify result status, add additional logs, or correct execution timing.

**Path Parameters:**
- `id` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the result to update

**Updateable Fields:**
- `status` (enum): "Passed", "Failed", "Blocked", "Skipped", or "Retest"
- `executedBy` (string): User ObjectId
- `startTime` (date): Execution start time
- `endTime` (date): Execution end time
- `logs` (string): Execution logs (can append or replace)
- `isActive` (boolean): Active status

**Common Use Cases:**
- Change status from Failed to Retest
- Add additional diagnostic logs
- Correct timing information
- Mark result as inactive
- Update executor information

**Note:** Duration is auto-recalculated when startTime or endTime is updated.

Deletes a test result. May be a soft delete (isActive = false) or hard delete based on configuration.

**Path Parameters:**
- `id` (string): MongoDB ObjectId of the result to delete

**Important Considerations:**
- Affects historical reporting
- May impact metrics and analytics
- Consider archiving instead of deleting
- May be subject to data retention policies

**Use Cases:**
- Remove duplicate results
- Clean up erroneous data
- Remove test results from cancelled runs
- Data cleanup operations

**Error Responses:**
- 404: Result not found
- 401: Unauthorized
- 403: Forbidden (may be restricted for audit compliance)

Generic search endpoint for results using prime-qa-commons search functionality. Enables flexible querying across result fields.

**Query Parameters:**
- `query` (string): General search term
- `status` (string): Filter by specific status
- `logs` (string): Search within execution logs
- `fields` (string): Comma-separated list of fields to search
- Additional filters supported by generic search

**Searchable Fields:**
- status
- logs
- executedBy
- And other indexed fields

**Use Cases:**
- Find all failed results
- Search for specific error patterns in logs
- Filter by executor
- Complex multi-field queries
- Debugging and troubleshooting

item.description: Test Result management endpoints. Results capture the outcome of test executions, including pass/fail status, timing information, and detailed execution logs.
